data.narrative:
Thea Energy, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Brian Berzin, has raised $137M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a broader nuclear energy comp universe of 11 peers (3 public, 8 private), including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and others, though narrow peer matching proved constrained.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, anchors to the $100M total raised proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding an implied valuation of $190M. Peer-median analysis was not applied; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (identical stage, adjacent stage, similar valuation tier) generated sufficient datapoints (≥3 required), making sector-wide median unreliable without risking misalignment on growth profile or technology maturity.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, with a median point of $190M. This range reflects limited methodological depth—reliance on a single applicable method—and underscores two material uncertainties: the absence of tightly matched peer comparables in the nascent advanced nuclear sector, and the model's sensitivity to stage-progression assumptions. Any upward or downward revision would require either additional nuclear-sector peers achieving comparable financing milestones or further operational data (deployment timelines, capital requirements, revenue traction) to calibrate stage-specific multiples more precisely.
